<!-- To verify freshness: alef verify --exit-code -->

# html-to-markdown

> Convert HTML to Markdown, Djot, or plain text. Rust core (`html-to-markdown-rs`) plus 15 generated packages, identical output on every runtime. The same engine ships under the [xberg.io](https://xberg.io) document intelligence stack.

## Get Started

- [Installation](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/installation/): Install the package for your language — Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go, Ruby, PHP, Java, C#, Elixir, R, C, WebAssembly, Swift, Dart, Kotlin Android, or Zig.
- [Usage](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/usage/): The `convert()` call and `ConversionResult` shape across bindings.
- [CLI](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/cli/): Command-line wrapper with every conversion option as a flag.

## Guides

- [Visitor pattern](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/visitor/): Intercept the DOM walk with `HtmlVisitor` to skip, replace, or preserve any element.
- [Table extraction](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/tables/): Structured `result.tables` with cells, spans, and header flags alongside the rendered Markdown.
- [Error handling](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/errors/): The `ConversionError` variants and how each binding surfaces them.
- [AI Coding Assistants](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/agent-skills/): Install the html-to-markdown plugin from the xberg-io/plugins marketplace for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Factory Droid, Copilot, and opencode.

## Concepts

- [Architecture](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/concepts/architecture/): Workspace layout, direct vs C-FFI bindings, feature flags, thread safety.
- [Conversion pipeline](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/concepts/pipeline/): The five stages of `convert()` — preprocess, parse, single-pass DOM walk, post-process, extract.
- [Plugin system](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/concepts/plugin-system/): The `HtmlVisitor` trait with 40 callbacks and `VisitResult::{Continue, Custom, Skip, PreserveHtml, Error}`.

## Reference

- [Configuration](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/configuration/): Every `ConversionOptions` field with type and default.
- [API reference](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/api-reference/): Generated API pages from the Rust source.
- [Language guides](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io/language-guides/): Per-language install notes, naming conventions, and idiom mapping.

## Installation snippets

- Rust: `html-to-markdown-rs = "3.6"` in Cargo.toml (Rust ≥ 1.85).
- Python: `pip install html-to-markdown` (Python ≥ 3.10).
- TypeScript: `npm install @xberg-io/html-to-markdown` (Node ≥ 18).
- Go: `go get github.com/xberg-io/html-to-markdown/packages/go/v3` (Go ≥ 1.26).
- Ruby: `gem 'html-to-markdown', '~> 3.6'` (Ruby ≥ 3.2).
- PHP: `pie install xberg-io/html-to-markdown` (native ext-php-rs extension; PHP ≥ 8.2).
- Java: Maven artifact `io.xberg:html-to-markdown:3.6.8` (Java ≥ 25).
- C#: `dotnet add package XbergIo.HtmlToMarkdown` (.NET 10).
- Elixir: `{:html_to_markdown, "~> 3.6"}` in mix.exs (Elixir ≥ 1.14).
- R: `install.packages("htmltomarkdown")` (R ≥ 4.2).
- WebAssembly: `npm install @xberg-io/html-to-markdown-wasm`.
- Swift: SwiftPM package `HtmlToMarkdown` (Swift ≥ 6.0).
- Dart: `dart pub add h2m` (Dart ≥ 3.11).
- Kotlin Android: Maven artifact `io.xberg:html-to-markdown-android` (Android minSdk 21; JVM Kotlin uses the Java package).
- Zig: package `html_to_markdown_rs` (Zig ≥ 0.16).
- C: shared library + header from GitHub Releases; HTM_H header, `htm_*` symbols.

## Xberg Ecosystem

- [Xberg](https://docs.xberg.io): Document intelligence: text, tables, metadata from 91+ formats with optional OCR
- [Xberg Enterprise](https://enterprise.xberg.io): Managed extraction API with SDKs, dashboards, and observability
- [crawlberg](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io): Web crawling and scraping with HTML to Markdown and headless-Chrome fallback
- [liter-llm](https://docs.liter-llm.xberg.io): Universal LLM API client with native bindings for 14 languages and 143 providers
- [tree-sitter-language-pack](https://docs.tree-sitter-language-pack.xberg.io): Tree-sitter grammars and code-intelligence primitives

## Generated CLI Inventory

- `html-to-markdown`: Convert HTML to Markdown
- `html-to-markdown mcp`: Run as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server.

## Generated MCP Inventory

- `convert_html`: Convert HTML to Markdown (or Djot/plain via config.output_format). Pass json:true for the full ConversionResult (content, tables, document structure, metadata, warnings). Pass config to control heading style, list formatting, escaping, preprocessing, image extraction, and more — see the input schema for every option.
- `extract_metadata`: Extract structured metadata from HTML as JSON: document title/description/keywords/author, Open Graph and Twitter Card tags, JSON-LD and microdata, plus header, link, and image inventories. Convenience over convert_html for metadata-only use.
